structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Fan Run Requirement
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode.

evidence_quote: The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Speed Ramp Limit
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Limit fan speed change to 10% per minute.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute.

evidence_quote: Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Mode Fan and SAT Control
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with heating coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When Zone State is Heating, fan speed and SAT setpoint are controlled based on heating-loop signal. For 100% to 50% signal, FANsp resets from MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed and SATsp is Heat_SAT. For 50% to 0% signal, FANsp is MinHeatSpeed and SATsp resets from Heat_SAT to active zone heating setpoint. Heating coil modulates to maintain SATsp. Cooling coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Fan speed and SAT setpoint follow heating-loop signal as described; heating coil modulates to SATsp; cooling coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. If there is a heating coil, when Zone State is Heating
1. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, FANsp is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed.
2. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, FANsp is MinHeatSpeed.
3. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT.
4. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to the active zone heating setpoint.
5. The heating coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp.
6. Cooling coil OFF

evidence_quote:
a. If there is a heating coil, when Zone State is Heating
1. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, FANsp is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed.
2. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, FANsp is MinHeatSpeed.
3. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT.
4. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to the active zone heating setpoint.
5. The heating coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp.
6. Cooling coil OFF

structured_payload_candidate.title: Deadband Mode Fan and Coil Control
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When Zone State is Deadband, FANsp shall be DeadbandSpeed. If DeadbandSpeed is zero, shut the fan off. Cooling coil OFF. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Fan speed set to DeadbandSpeed (shut off if zero); cooling and heating coils OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
b. When Zone State is Deadband
1. FANsp shall be DeadbandSpeed. If DeadbandSpeed is zero, shut the fan off.
2. Cooling coil OFF
3. Heating coil OFF

evidence_quote:
b. When Zone State is Deadband
1. FANsp shall be DeadbandSpeed. If DeadbandSpeed is zero, shut the fan off.
2. Cooling coil OFF
3. Heating coil OFF

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Mode Fan and SAT Control
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with cooling coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When Zone State is Cooling, fan speed and SAT setpoint are controlled based on cooling-loop signal. For 0% to 50% signal, FANsp is MinCoolSpeed and SATsp resets from active zone cooling setpoint to Cool_SAT. For 50% to 100% signal, FANsp resets from MinCoolSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed and SATsp is Cool_SAT. Cooling coil modulates to maintain SATsp. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Fan speed and SAT setpoint follow cooling-loop signal as described; cooling coil modulates to SATsp; heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
c. If there is a cooling coil, when Zone State is Cooling
1. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, FANsp is MinCoolSpeed.
2. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, FANsp is reset from MinCoolSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.
3. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, SATsp is reset from the active zone cooling setpoint to Cool_SAT.
4. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, SATsp is Cool_SAT.
5. The cooling coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp.
6. Heating coil OFF

evidence_quote:
c. If there is a cooling coil, when Zone State is Cooling
1. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, FANsp is MinCoolSpeed.
2. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, FANsp is reset from MinCoolSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.
3. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, SATsp is reset from the active zone cooling setpoint to Cool_SAT.
4. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, SATsp is Cool_SAT.
5. The cooling coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp.
6. Heating coil OFF

structured_payload_candidate.title: Maintenance Interval Alarm for Fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 4 alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Level 4 alarm; reset counter on acknowledgment.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.

evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Status Mismatch Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan alarm indicated by status different from command for 15 seconds. Commanded on, status off: Level 2. Commanded off, status on: Level 4.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Fan status differs from command for 15 seconds.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Alarm Level 2 if commanded on/status off; Level 4 if commanded off/status on.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds.
a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2
b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4

evidence_quote:
Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds.
a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2
b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4

structured_payload_candidate.title: Filter Pressure Drop Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05") for 10 minutes when fan speed exceeds 20% of MaxCoolSpeed: Level 4. Alarm limit varies with fan speed: DPx = DP100 * (x)^1.4.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Filter DP > max(alarm limit, 12.5 Pa) for 10 minutes and fan speed > 20% MaxCoolSpeed.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Filter DP > max(DPx, 12.5 Pa) for 10 min and fan speed > 20% MaxCoolSpeed
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when fan speed exceeds 20% of MaxCoolSpeed: Level 4. The alarm limit shall vary with fan speed as follows: 𝐷𝑃𝑥=𝐷𝑃100(𝑥)1.4

evidence_quote: Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when fan speed exceeds 20% of MaxCoolSpeed: Level 4. The alarm limit shall vary with fan speed as follows: 𝐷𝑃𝑥=𝐷𝑃100(𝑥)1.4

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU Operating State Definition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The OS of each FCU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating-coil control valve and cooling-coil control valve in accordance with Table 5.20.6.2.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Determine OS based on valve positions: OS#1 Heating (HC>0, CC=0), OS#2 No Heating or Cooling (HC=0, CC=0), OS#3 Cooling (HC=0, CC>0), OS#4 Unknown (otherwise).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The OS of each FCU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating-coil control valve and cooling-coil control valve in accordance with Table 5.20.6.2 and Figure 5.20.6.2.

evidence_quote: The OS of each FCU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating-coil control valve and cooling-coil control valve in accordance with Table 5.20.6.2 and Figure 5.20.6.2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Required Points
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The following points must be available to the AFDD routines for each FCU: SAT, RAT (if present), SATsp, HC, CC, FS.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
The following points must be available to the AFDD routines for each FCU:
a. SAT = supply air temperature
b. RAT = return air temperature (if present)
c. SATsp = supply air temperature setpoint
d. HC = heating-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ HC ≤ 100%
e. CC = cooling-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ CC ≤ 100%
f. FS = fan-speed command; 0% ≤ FS ≤ 100%

evidence_quote:
The following points must be available to the AFDD routines for each FCU:
a. SAT = supply air temperature
b. RAT = return air temperature (if present)
c. SATsp = supply air temperature setpoint
d. HC = heating-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ HC ≤ 100%
e. CC = cooling-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ CC ≤ 100%
f. FS = fan-speed command; 0% ≤ FS ≤ 100%

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Rolling Averages Calculation
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling of SAT, RAT (if fitted), and ΔOS (number of OS changes in previous 60 minutes) must be continuously calculated. Operator can adjust averaging window and sampling period.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate SATavg, RATavg, ΔOS as rolling averages.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling of the following point values; operator shall have the ability to adjust the averaging window and sampling period for each point independently.
1. SAT avg = rolling average of supply air temperature
2. RAT avg = rolling average of return air temperature (if fitted)
3. ΔOS = number of changes in OS during the previous 60 minutes (moving window)

evidence_quote:
Five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling of the following point values; operator shall have the ability to adjust the averaging window and sampling period for each point independently.
1. SAT avg = rolling average of supply air temperature
2. RAT avg = rolling average of return air temperature (if fitted)
3. ΔOS = number of changes in OS during the previous 60 minutes (moving window)

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Internal Variables Default Values
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Default values for internal variables: ΔTSF=0.5°C (1°F), εSAT=1°C (2°F), εRAT=1°C (2°F), ΔOSmax=7, ModeDelay=30 min, AlarmDelay=30 min, TestModeDelay=120 min. All adjustable by operator.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Table 5.20.6.5 FCU Internal Variables
Variable Name Description Default Value
ΔTSF Temperature rise across supply fan 0.5°C (1°F)
εSAT Temperature error threshold for SAT sensor 1°C (2°F)
εRAT Temperature error threshold for RAT sensor 1°C (2°F)
ΔOSmax Maximum number of changes in Operating State during the previous 60 minutes (moving window) 7
ModeDelay Time in minutes to suspend Fault Condition evaluation after a change in mode 30
AlarmDelay Time in minutes that a Fault Condition must persist before triggering an alarm 30
TestModeDelay Time in minutes that Test Mode is enabled 120

evidence_quote:
Table 5.20.6.5 FCU Internal Variables
Variable Name Description Default Value
ΔTSF Temperature rise across supply fan 0.5°C (1°F)
εSAT Temperature error threshold for SAT sensor 1°C (2°F)
εRAT Temperature error threshold for RAT sensor 1°C (2°F)
ΔOSmax Maximum number of changes in Operating State during the previous 60 minutes (moving window) 7
ModeDelay Time in minutes to suspend Fault Condition evaluation after a change in mode 30
AlarmDelay Time in minutes that a Fault Condition must persist before triggering an alarm 30
TestModeDelay Time in minutes that Test Mode is enabled 120

structured_payload_candidate.title: FC#1: Too Many Changes in Operating State
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault condition FC#1: ΔOS > ΔOSmax. Applies to OS#1-#4. Possible diagnosis: unstable control due to poorly tuned loop or mechanical problem.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
FC #1 Equation ΔOS > ΔOS MAX
Applies to OS #1 – #4 Description Too many changes in Operating State
Possible Diagnosis Unstable control due to poorly tuned loop or mechanical problem

Possible diagnoses include SAT sensor error, cooling coil valve leaking/stuck open, heating coil valve stuck closed/actuator failure, fouled/undersized heating coil, HW temperature too low/unavailable, gas/electric heat unavailable, DX cooling stuck on.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Condition Evaluation by OS
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In OS#1 (Heating), evaluate FC#1 and FC#2. In OS#2 (Deadband), evaluate FC#5 (heating only) and FC#4 (cooling only). In OS#3 (Cooling), evaluate FC#1 and FC#3. In OS#4 (other), evaluate FC#1.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. In OS#1 (Heating), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated:
1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS
2. FC#2: SAT too low in full heating
b. In OS#2 (Deadband), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated:
1. FC#5: Temperature drop across inactive heating coil (heating only FCU
2. FC#4: Temperature drop across inactive cooling coil (cooling only FCU
c. In OS#3 (Cooling), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated:
1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS
2. FC#3: SAT too high in full cooling
d. In OS#4 (other), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated:
1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS

evidence_quote:
a. In OS#1 (Heating), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated:
1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS
2. FC#2: SAT too low in full heating
b. In OS#2 (Deadband), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated:
1. FC#5: Temperature drop across inactive heating coil (heating only FCU
2. FC#4: Temperature drop across inactive cooling coil (cooling only FCU
c. In OS#3 (Cooling), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated:
1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS
2. FC#3: SAT too high in full cooling
d. In OS#4 (other), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated:
1. FC#1: Too many changes in OS

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Alarm Suppression by Operator
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For each FCU, the operator shall be able to suppress the alarm for any fault condition.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: For each FCU, the operator shall be able to suppress the alarm for any fault condition.

evidence_quote: For each FCU, the operator shall be able to suppress the alarm for any fault condition.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Evaluation Suspension Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended when FCU is not operating, and for a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode of any Zone Group served by the FCU.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: FCU not operating or mode change in served Zone Group.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions:
a. When FCU is not operating
b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode or Cooldown Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the FCU.

evidence_quote:
Evaluation of fault conditions shall be suspended under the following conditions:
a. When FCU is not operating
b. For a period of ModeDelay minutes following a change in mode (e.g., from Warmup Mode or Cooldown Mode to Occupied Mode) of any Zone Group served by the FCU.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Condition Evaluation Restriction
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault conditions that are not applicable to the current OS shall not be evaluated.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Current OS does not match fault condition applicability.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Do not evaluate inapplicable fault conditions.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fault conditions that are not applicable to the current OS shall not be evaluated.

evidence_quote: Fault conditions that are not applicable to the current OS shall not be evaluated.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Alarm Delay Requirement
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A fault condition that evaluates as true must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: A fault condition that evaluates as true must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.

evidence_quote: A fault condition that evaluates as true must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Test Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 for TestModeDelay minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.

evidence_quote: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Condition Alarm Reporting
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When a fault condition is reported to the operator, it shall be a Level 3 alarm and shall include the description of the fault and the list of possible diagnoses from Table 5.20.6.6.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Level 3 alarm with description and possible diagnoses.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When a fault condition is reported to the operator, it shall be a Level 3 alarm and shall include the description of the fault and the list of possible diagnoses from Table 5.20.6.6.

evidence_quote: When a fault condition is reported to the operator, it shall be a Level 3 alarm and shall include the description of the fault and the list of possible diagnoses from Table 5.20.6.6.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Testing/Commissioning Overrides for Valves
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Provide software switches that interlock to a CHW and hot-water plant level to force HW valve full open, force HW valve full closed, force CHW valve full open, and force CHW valve full closed.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify software switches can force valves full open/closed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Provide software switches that interlock to a CHW and hot-water plant level to
a. force HW valve full open if there is a hot-water coil,
b. force HW valve full closed if there is a hot-water coil,
c. force CHW valve full open if there is a CHW coil, and
d. force CHW valve full closed if there is a CHW coil.

evidence_quote:
Provide software switches that interlock to a CHW and hot-water plant level to
a. force HW valve full open if there is a hot-water coil,
b. force HW valve full closed if there is a hot-water coil,
c. force CHW valve full open if there is a CHW coil, and
d. force CHW valve full closed if there is a CHW coil.

structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with chilled-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: All chilled-water reset requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxCoolSpeed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxCoolSpeed.

evidence_quote: All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxCoolSpeed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chilled-Water Reset Request Generation
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with chilled-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SAT is 10°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests. Else if SAT is 5°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 2 requests. Else if CHW valve position > 95%, send 1 request until CHW valve position < 85%. Else if CHW valve position < 95%, send 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Various conditions based on SAT error and CHW valve position.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
b. If the supply air temperature is 10°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests,
c. Else if the supply air temperature is 5°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 2 requests,
d. Else if the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 85%.
e. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote:
b. If the supply air temperature is 10°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests,
c. Else if the supply air temperature is 5°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 2 requests,
d. Else if the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 85%.
e. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chiller Plant Request Generation

structured_payload_candidate.summary: Send chiller plant request: if CHW valve position > 95%, send 1 request until CHW valve position < 10%. Else if CHW valve position < 95%, send 0 requests.

structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. If the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 10%.
b. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

b. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hot-Water Reset Requests Suppression

structured_payload_candidate.summary: All hot-water reset requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxHeatSpeed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxHeatSpeed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hot-Water Reset Request Generation
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit with hot-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SAT is 17°C (30°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 3 requests. Else if SAT is 8°C (15°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 2 requests. Else if HW valve position > 95%, send 1 request until HW valve position < 85%. Else if HW valve position < 95%, send 0 requests.

c. Else if the supply air temperature is 8°C (15°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 2 requests.
d. Else if HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 85%.
e. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

b. If the supply air temperature is 17°C (30°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 3 requests.
c. Else if the supply air temperature is 8°C (15°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 2 requests.
d. Else if HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 85%.
e. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
